text:
There was once a girl named Willow who lived in a little village. She loved the woods behind her house, especially in the autumn when the leaves turned silver. Every morning she would sneak there, finding comfort in the rustling leaves crunching underfoot. One day her favorite spot fell into disrepair. Trees fell, a path was lost, and a gnarly old oak fell victim to lightning.

Willow felt sad whenever she couldn’t pass by there anymore. So she volunteered, determined to restore it. She spent weekends digging, transplanting roots, painting wooden boards. When the oak finally got its new home, it seemed even greener. She was thrilled.

Every Saturday, Willow walked there, hoping no one else would notice how much it meant to her. One sunny autumn morning something wonderful happened. As she approached, she noticed daws chirping, the wind rustling leaves, and a gentle glow from the old oak. It was more beautiful than she remembered. Embarassed
